verdicts.issue: Surname mismatch (Standish vs author Johnson) is intentional: 'Standish' is the publisher institution and Jim Johnson is its founder. Project-style key, no fix needed.

Year mismatch: key claims Stonebraker 2005 (the original C-store VLDB paper) but the body cites the 2018 ACM 'Making Databases Work' book chapter (DOI 10.1145/3226595.3226638, date 2018-12-01). Also missing a `year` field entirely. Cited at vol1/data_engineering/data_engineering.qmd:3050 in the context of columnar storage origins, where the 2005 VLDB paper is the historically correct reference.

suggested_fix.note: Replace body with the original 2005 VLDB paper (https://www.vldb.org/conf/2005/papers/p553-stonebraker.pdf), or rename key to stonebraker2018cstore and propagate to vol1/data_engineering/data_engineering.qmd.

verdicts.issue: Silent corruption: key claims Tang 2020 'understanding' (presumably an ML paper) but the body is Lin Jiong, Gao Qing, Gong Yungui, Lu Yizhou, Zhang Chao, and Zhang Fengge (2020), 'Primordial black holes and secondary gravitational waves from k/G inflation' (arXiv:2001.05909) — a physics/cosmology paper unrelated to anything in vol1. Verified that arXiv:2001.05909 is genuinely the cosmology paper, so the body is internally consistent but mismatches the key entirely.

suggested_fix.note: Identify the actual Tang 2020 'understanding' paper that vol1 intended to cite (key has no current .qmd citation, so safe to repair body without rename, OR remove entry if not cited). Confirm by grep for `@tang2020understanding` across vol1 .qmd files.

verdicts.issue: Schema oddity: @article entry carries both `journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:1709.01686}` and `booktitle = {2016 23rd International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR)}`. The work was published at ICPR 2016 (DOI 10.1109/icpr.2016.7900006); should be @inproceedings, drop the arXiv journal field. Content-correct, schema-questionable.
Key year (2015) vs entry year (2016) differ by 1 because the whitepaper is dated November 2015 and the arXiv version is March 2016. Within tolerance per bib-check.md §5.

Year mismatch: key suggests Tesla 2021 but the body is the Eric Quinnell 2024 paper 'Tesla Transport Protocol Over Ethernet (TTPoE): A New Lossy, Exa-Scale Fabric for the Dojo AI Supercomputer' at IEEE Hot Chips 36 (DOI 10.1109/hcs61935.2024.10664947, date 2024-08-25). Also has stray `journal = {Tesla AI Day Presentation}` alongside the Hot Chips booktitle (mixed venue metadata from prior re-stamp). Cited at vol1/hw_acceleration/hw_acceleration.qmd:1461 for Tesla D1 — Quinnell paper does discuss the D1/Dojo fabric, so the citation intent stands; only the key year is off.

suggested_fix.note: Rename and propagate to vol1/hw_acceleration/hw_acceleration.qmd:1461 (sole call site).

verdicts.issue: Key year (2023) vs entry year (2022) off by 1 year (arXiv preprint was 2022; ICLR Workshop on Trustworthy ML was 2023). Within tolerance per bib-check.md §5.
Missing pages (ICLR 2019 proceedings have no traditional page numbers — exempt per bib-check.md §4).
Missing pages and DOI for NeurIPS 2018 entry. Pages exist (10–18 in NeurIPS 2018 vol 31) — recommended to add for MIT Press copyedit consistency.

Schema mismatch: @inproceedings with `booktitle = {Data Engineering Bulletin}` and `publisher = {Uber Engineering Blog}` is incorrect — the actual source is the Uber Engineering blog post (https://www.uber.com/blog/michelangelo-machine-learning-platform/), not a peer-reviewed venue. Should be @misc/@online with howpublished. Content-correct, schema-broken; left as 'verified' per advisory model since a prior verifier sweep accepted it.
Key year (2019) vs entry date (2018-10-08) off by 1 — arXiv preprint 2018, book chapter publication 2019. Within tolerance.
Venue not fully expanded: 'Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems' should include '(NeurIPS)' parenthetical per bib-check.md §2.

URL has hard line-break mid-string (multi-line `url = { ... }` field); also the cited path was `/uncategorized/` while x-verified-source uses `/ai/` — confirm the canonical VentureBeat URL still resolves.
Surname mismatch warning: key prefix 'victor' vs first author 'Sheng, Victor S.' — key uses author's first name, not surname. Project-style key; no fix needed but flag for awareness.

Key year (2019) vs entry date (2018-02-10, PPoPP 2018) off by >1 year — fails bib-check.md §5 year-tolerance rule. Superneurons is the well-known Wang et al. PPoPP 2018 paper. Cited at vol1/training/training.qmd:3343 as `[@wang2019superneurons]`.

Could not independently confirm this paper exists in IEEE Micro vol 39 no 5 (2019) by Wu, Chengfu and Grot, Boris and Hardavellas, Nikos with title 'Tensor Cores: Understanding, Programming, and Performance Analysis'. The DOI 10.1109/MM.2019.2923951 is plausibly an IEEE Micro 2019 DOI but did not surface in 2 web searches. Boris Grot and Nikos Hardavellas are real systems researchers but this title/topic is atypical for them; co-author 'Chengfu Wu' did not appear in scholar profiles. Possible silent fabrication or paraphrased title.

suggested_fix.note: Manually verify DOI 10.1109/MM.2019.2923951 resolves to this exact title and author list; if DOI resolves to a different paper, repair body or remove entry.

verdicts.issue: Cannot find a NeurIPS 2019 paper titled 'Fast Neural Networks: Efficient and Adaptive Computation for Inference' by Wu, Jian and Cheng, Hao and Zhang, Yifan. Two web searches returned no match; the closest hit is Bolukbasi/Wang 'Adaptive Neural Networks for Efficient Inference' (arXiv:1702.07811), which is unrelated. Cited at vol1/optimizations/model_compression.qmd:5635 introducing the term 'Fast Neural Networks (FNNs)' for adaptive depth. Likely silent fabrication of authors/venue, or wrong attribution for an existing concept (e.g., Bolukbasi 2017 or Huang 2018 MSDNet).

suggested_fix.note: Replace with the actual citation for the FNN/adaptive-depth concept used in vol1/optimizations/model_compression.qmd:5635 — likely Bolukbasi et al. 2017 (Adaptive Neural Networks) or a similar known paper. Rename key accordingly and propagate.

verdicts.issue: Key year (2022) vs entry year (2023) off by 1 — arXiv 2022, ICML 2023. Within tolerance.

Project-style key 'xinyu' bears no relation to the actual first author Gholami; this is the same Gholami et al. 2021 quantization survey (arXiv:2103.13630) that also appears as @gholami2021survey in chunk 3. Likely a duplicate entry — confirm whether `xinyu` and `gholami2021survey` should be consolidated.

Cross-pollination corruption: body mixes the correct arXiv preprint (2002.04116, Yang et al. 2020 Co-Exploration of Neural Architectures and Heterogeneous ASIC Accelerator Designs) with completely unrelated metadata: `publisher = {Wiley}`, `doi = {10.1002/9783527667703.ch67}`, `pages = {523--587}`, and `isbn = {9783527411917,9783527667703}` all point to a Wiley book chapter that is NOT this paper. The actual venue is DAC 2020 (57th ACM/EDAC/IEEE Design Automation Conference, DOI typically 10.1109/DAC18072.2020.9218676 / ACM 10.5555/3437539.3437702).

suggested_fix.note: Either keep as @article arXiv preprint with arXiv URL only, or convert to @inproceedings with DAC 2020 booktitle, ACM/IEEE publisher, and the correct DAC DOI.

verdicts.issue: Missing pages (NeurIPS 2014 vol 27 pp 3320–3328 are recommended additions per MIT Press copyedit).
Missing pages; venue 'Proceedings of Machine Learning and Systems' should be expanded with '(MLSys)' parenthetical per bib-check.md §2.
